title: Racosperma papulosum
intro: Racosperma papulosum, also known as the Queensland wattle, is a small tree or shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Racosperma papulosum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its skin-soothing and antimicrobial effects.
taxonomy: Racosperma papulosum is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It was previously classified as Acacia papulosum, but was later reclassified as Racosperma papulosum. The reclassification was made based on the phylogenetic analysis of the Acacia genus.
